Member-only story

“Good debt” is still debt. The label doesn’t change the math.

2 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Image Generated By Ai
• The Label Was Invented to Make You Comfortable
“Good debt” versus “bad debt” is a useful teaching tool, but it’s still marketing language for an obligation you have to repay with interest, on a schedule, regardless of how your life goes that year.
2. A Mortgage Is Good Debt — Until the Payment Eats Your Options
Owning property is a reasonable goal. A mortgage that consumes so much of your income that you can’t save, invest, or absorb a bad month isn’t “good debt” — it’s just debt with better PR.
3. Student Loans Don’t Care About Your Job Market
Education debt gets called “an investment in yourself,” which is true right up until graduation, when the loan still demands payment…
